name: Actix Web
what_it_does: Rust's most popular web framework by GitHub stars. Built on Tokio with an actor system heritage. Provides extractors, middleware, WebSocket support, streaming, and HTTP/2. One of the fastest web frameworks in the TechEmpower benchmarks. Mature ecosystem with more plugins and examples than axum, though axum is growing faster.
use_cases: Build high-performance Rust HTTP API backends for agent services with mature middleware ecosystem
Implement WebSocket-based real-time agent communication endpoints with Actix's WebSocket support
Create agent microservices with Actix's rich middleware ecosystem (CORS, compression, rate limiting, auth)
Build streaming response endpoints for LLM token delivery using Actix's streaming body support
Develop background service workers alongside HTTP handlers using Actix's actor system integration
not_for: New Rust projects choosing between axum and actix-web — axum is now preferred for its ergonomics and Tower integration
Teams valuing minimal dependencies — Actix has more dependencies than axum for its actor system features
Applications needing Tower middleware ecosystem — axum integrates Tower natively; Actix uses its own middleware traits
best_when: You're maintaining an existing Actix application or need its specific WebSocket/actor system features; or prefer its more established ecosystem over axum's newer but growing one.
avoid_when: Starting a new Rust web service — axum has better ergonomics, Tower integration, and is growing faster; choose axum for greenfield projects.

auth.notes: Web framework — auth implemented via middleware. actix-web-httpauth crate for HTTP auth middleware.

pricing.notes: MIT/Apache-2.0 dual license. Community-maintained after original author's retirement controversy.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits built-in. Consistently top 3 in TechEmpower plaintext benchmarks.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Extractor errors return HTTP error responses with cause. ResponseError trait for custom error handling. Middleware error chaining propagates cleanly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: HTTP framework — idempotency is application responsibility. App state is thread-safe via Arc.
known_agent_gotchas: Actix uses a custom middleware API (Transform, Service) different from Tower — Tower middleware from the axum/hyper ecosystem is not directly compatible
web::Data<T> for shared state requires T: Send + Sync + 'static — non-Send types require wrapping in Mutex or Arc<Mutex>
Actix runs on its own thread pool (not Tokio by default in older versions) — in actix-web 4.x it uses Tokio, but mixing Tokio code requires careful compatibility
Handler functions are async and return types implementing Responder — implementing Responder for custom types requires implementing the trait correctly
WebSocket upgrading closes the HTTP connection and switches protocols — the upgrade handler must consume the HttpRequest and Payload to complete the WebSocket handshake
actix-web 3.x to 4.x had breaking changes (Data registration, middleware API) — code from pre-4.x tutorials requires updates; check version carefully

security_score_components.security_notes: Rust memory safety eliminates buffer overflows. TLS via rustls or openssl. Secure by default with proper middleware. Validate all user input via typed extractors.
